'''Richard Fa'aoso''' (born 8 May 1984), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Richie"''',<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-02-04/richie-fa27aoso-announces-his-nrl-retirement-because-of-neck-i/5237770 |title=Fa'aoso announces NRL retirement |date=4 February 2014|website=Abc.net.au|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref> and '''"Fuss"''',<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/481394/coming-of-age/|title=Coming of age|date=26 March 2008|website=Anc.net.au|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref> is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. He played as a {{rlp|pr}} and played for the [[Penrith Panthers]], [[Sydney Roosters]], [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Melbourne Storm]] and the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Fa'aoso is of [[Australians|Australian]], [[Tongans|Tongan]] and [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent. His father Tongan and Samoan, while his mother is Australian of European descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.optuszoo.com.au/2013/11/25/nrl-star-id-never-hurt-wife-kids/ |title=NRL star: 'I'd never hurt wife, kids' |publisher=Optus Zoo |access-date=11 October 2017 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131128164306/http://news.optuszoo.com.au/2013/11/25/nrl-star-id-never-hurt-wife-kids/ |archive-date=28 November 2013}}</ref>

He was educated at [[Wavell State High School]]. Fa'aoso played his junior football for Norths Aspley before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]].

==Playing career==
===Penrith Panthers===

In round 14 of the [[2004 NRL season]], Fa'aoso made his [[NRL]] début for Penrith against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. Fa'aoso played for Penrith in their preliminary final defeat against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]].

===Sydney Roosters===
In 2005, he joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] and played 21 games for them during the year.

===Castleford Tigers===
In 2006, Fa'aoso played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], who signed him after their promotion in 2005.

===Parramatta Eels===
In 2007, he returned to Australia, signing with the [[Parramatta Eels]] for three years. He was touted as a replacement for departing Parramatta player [[Michael Vella]] who left the Eels to join Super League club [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2007. Faʻaoso only played one first grade game for the club which was Parramatta's round 4 victory over [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]].

===Newcastle Knights===
After 2007, Fa'aoso was released to sign a two-year deal with the recently cleaned out, [[Brian Smith (rugby league, born 1954)|Brian Smith]]-coached [[Newcastle Knights]]. During 2008, Fa'aoso became a permanent first-grader for Newcastle, earning a new three-year contract for the Newcastle club after making 45 appearances and scoring seven tries for the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/55611/Default.aspx|title=Knights put faith in Fa'aoso|website=Nrl.com|access-date=11 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171011073521/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/55611/Default.aspx|archive-date=11 October 2017|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Fa'aoso made another 46 appearances, scoring three tries during 2010 and 2011, making the Newcastle side the club that he spent the most time with.

In 2012 [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] took over as coach of the Newcastle outfit and at the halfway stage of the season Fa'aoso had only made eight appearances for the team. He was told that he wouldn't be re-signed for 2013 after 99 games and wouldn't be stopped if he were to find a new club to join mid-season before 30 June transfer deadline.

===Melbourne Storm===
On 27 June 2012, Fa'aoso traveled to [[Melbourne]] to have a fitness test in order to sign with the [[Melbourne Storm]] for the remainder of the 2012 season. He officially signed with the Storm later that afternoon. The Storm's general manager of Football Operations, Frank Ponissi, said the signing of Fa'aoso was a welcome addition to their NRL squad : "We see Richie's signing as a chance to bring some further experience and strength to our roster."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Storm-adds-depth-to-NRL-squad/58113 |title=Storm adds depth to NRL squad - Melbourne Storm |access-date=2012-07-10 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120629233516/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Storm-adds-depth-to-NRL-squad/58113 |archive-date=29 June 2012 |df=dmy-all }}</ref> Fa'aoso left the Knights as a fan favourite, playing 99 games and scoring 12 tries. Fa'aoso said about the move ''“I have had a ball at the Knights and I will always have great mates here, I thank the Knights for the opportunity and to everyone for their efforts; the coaches, my teammates, the trainers, just everyone at the Club for all they have done for me. Wayne (Bennett) was great to give me a chance at the start of the season and I am disappointed I was unable to make the most of it. I also want to say thank you to all of the Knights fans for your support. The time is right for me to move on and I wish the Knights all the best for the rest of the season and beyond.”''<ref>[https://archive.today/20121230133010/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-release-Faaoso/58093 Knights release Fa’aoso - Newcastle Knights<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

On 30 September 2012, Fa'aoso came off the bench in the [[Melbourne Storm]]'s [[2012 NRL Grand Final]] win over the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. He played a total of six games for the club.

===Manly Warringah Sea Eagles===
On 10 July 2012, just 13 days after signing with Melbourne, Fa'aoso signed a two-year deal with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] starting in 2013, despite not yet having played a game for the Melbourne Storm.

Towards the end of the [[2013 NRL season]], Fa'aoso broke his neck. In February 2014, doctors advised him never to play [[Rugby league]] again. He subsequently announced his retirement from [[Rugby league]].<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140227091808/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8793975 Fa'aoso exit leaves Manly light up front<!-- Bot generated title -->]</ref>

===Parramatta Eels second stint===
In September 2014, Fa'aoso was cleared for a shock comeback to the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] from his broken neck.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/richie-faaoso-cleared-to-return-to-nrl-less-than-a-year-after-breaking-his-neck-20140903-10byzt.html|title=Richie Fa'aoso cleared to return to NRL less than a year after breaking his neck|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=3 September 2014|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]}}</ref> On 30 October 2014, he signed a one-year contract with the [[Parramatta Eels]] starting in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/10/30/eels_announce_five_signings_for_2015.html|title=Eels Announce Five Signings For 2015|website=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/ritchie-faaoso-confirms-return-from-broken-neck-signing-with-parramatta-for-next-season/story-e6frf3tc-1227079499583?from=public_rss&nk=4f6c065ea0f2346074142901b5997c30|title=Fa'aoso to resurrect career with Eels|date=3 October 2014|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref> However the NRL did not register the contract, after Fa'aoso could not secure adequate insurance cover for his return.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/66522594/nrl-refuse-to-register-richie-faaosos-parramatta-contract-over-insurance-concerns|title=NRL refuse to register Richie Fa'aoso's Parramatta contract over insurance concerns|website=Stuff.co.nz|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref>

On 2 April 2015, Fa'aoso was cleared to return to the playing field.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/richie-faaoso-cleared-to-return/tabid/10874/newsid/85058/default.aspx|title=Richie Fa'aoso cleared to return|website=Nrl.com|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref> He made his return in round 7 of the [[2015 NRL season]] against his former team Newcastle.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/richie-faaoso-returns-for-parramatta-eels-569-days-after-breaking-his-neck/story-fni3ga7r-1227309642090|title=Fa'aoso returns, 569 days since breaking his neck|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref> Shortly after getting on the field, Fa'aoso suffered a concussion from a head knock and was taken off, he subsequently failed the concussion test and was ruled out for the remainder of the match.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/video-nasty-head-clash-in-eels-prop-richie-faaosos-nrl-return-against-newcastle/story-e6frf3tc-1227310807852|title=Video: Nasty head clash in Fa'aoso return|date=19 April 2015|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=11 October 2017}}</ref>
In October 2015, Fa'aoso again announced his retirement.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2015/10/05/nrl-farewells-retiring-players/ |title=NRL farewells retiring players |publisher=Parramatta Eels |date=5 October 2015 |access-date=18 September 2021}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Fa'aoso made his international début for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in 2006, playing 4 tests.

In August 2008, Fa'aoso was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]],<ref name="Tug-of-war over Mason">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3935853,00.html|title=Tug-of-war over Mason|publisher=Sky Sports|date=5 August 2008|access-date=2008-08-06}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga]] squad.<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref>

Fa'aoso was again selected for the Tongan national team in 2009 for the [[2009 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]]. He was named [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] player of the year for 2009.<ref name="Hayne named Player of the Year">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992|title=Hayne named Player of the Year|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=10 November 2009|access-date=2010-04-19}}</ref>

On 20 April 2013, Fa'aoso played for Tonga in their Pacific Rugby League International match with [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].

On 2 May 2015, Fa'aoso made his 10th appearance for Tonga in his country's clash against Pacific rivals Samoa in the 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]].
